webextension-polyfill — vendored
=================================

Upstream:   https://github.com/mozilla/webextension-polyfill
Version:    0.12.0
Source URL: https://unpkg.com/webextension-polyfill@0.12.0/dist/browser-polyfill.js
Reviewed:   2026-06-04
License:    MPL-2.0

Upstream SHA-256:
  cfc53c3525587467c783ef1c6fdefbaf1d60ed197ea2fe0d45899b18069cf496

Vendored SHA-256 (browser-polyfill.js as committed):
  d42e49ea624d66450a3d7cacba5b2478e1f20551285e7656d4c8a01b0ab5dea8

Modification
------------
A small ESM adapter is appended after the upstream UMD bytes. The IIFE
sets `globalThis.browser` in non-AMD/CommonJS environments; the appendix
re-exports it as the module default so feature code can write:

  import browser from '../vendor/browser-polyfill.js';

The upstream bytes above the appendix are byte-identical to the release.

Why the polyfill (and not raw chrome.*)
---------------------------------------
Firefox MV3 ships `browser.*` (promise-based) natively but `chrome.*` only
in callback form. Chrome ships `chrome.*` in both callback and (for many
APIs) promise form. The polyfill gives us one promise-based surface across
both. We pay 30KB in code size for ~zero per-call ceremony in feature code.

Update procedure
----------------
1. Curl new file from the upstream release URL above.
2. Compare diff against upstream-recorded SHA; verify what changed.
3. Re-append the ESM adapter (`export default globalThis.browser;`).
4. Record the new SHA-256s and date here.
